refactor_notes: Three concrete violations against architecture.md. (1) Layer boundary: the create_bug route handler directly calls db.add(), db.commit(), db.refresh() and instantiates Bug() with status='open' hardcoded -- all three are application/infrastructure concerns that architecture.md §Pattern Proposals assigns to BugService (create logic, status initialization) and BugRepository (data access). The HTTP layer must not own persistence. Refactor: extract app/services/bug_service.py with a create(title, description)->Bug method and app/repositories/bug_repository.py with add()/get() methods; have the route delegate to BugService. (2) Dead code + DRY violation: ALLOWED_STATUSES is defined in models.py but is never referenced; the CheckConstraint hardcodes the same three values as a literal string on the very next line. Either use ALLOWED_STATUSES in the constraint expression or remove the tuple. Architecture.md §Risks cites R2 mitigation requiring consistent enforcement of the allowed-status set. (3) No server-side title validation: architecture.md §Architectural Concerns Mapping lists server-side validation (title, description) as owned by BugService + API middleware. The HTML required attribute is client-only; an empty or whitespace-only title reaches the DB unchecked. The refactor should add a guard in BugService.create() that raises a ValueError for blank title, caught by API error middleware and returned as a 422 with a field-naming message per nfrs.md Preferences. None of these changes alter what the outer-boundary Playwright tests at POST /bugs assert.
